mechanism_narrative: Higher 3-month growth in retail sales signals strengthening household demand and near-term revenue momentum for index constituents, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:RSAFS July 2005 vintage value was 357013 million dollars
failure_condition: Reject if higher 3-month RSAFS growth has Rank IC below 0.03 over two consecutive 3-year rolling windows or triggered long signals underperform the unconditional 20-day return.

mechanism_narrative: When the federal funds rate crosses above 3.0% while its 6-month change is positive, tighter discount rates and rising financing costs reduce equity valuations, producing negative S&P 500 returns over 60 trading days.
candidate_trigger: FEDFUNDS crosses above 3.0% and 6-month change is positive
evidence_refs: fact:FEDFUNDS July 2005 vintage value was 3.26 percent
failure_condition: Reject if 60-day forward returns after the trigger are positive in more than 60% of cases or are not below unconditional returns with t-test p <= 0.05.

mechanism_narrative: Joint increases in CPI and the federal funds rate identify an inflation-fighting tightening regime in which policy risk dominates nominal revenue growth, producing negative S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: CPIAUCSL 3-month change is positive and FEDFUNDS 3-month change is positive
failure_condition: Reject if the CPIAUCSL-FEDFUNDS interaction coefficient is not negative in walk-forward tests or if triggered 20-day returns exceed baseline returns.

mechanism_narrative: Payroll growth combined with a falling unemployment rate signals broad income gains without visible labor-market stress, increasing expected earnings and producing positive S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: PAYEMS 3-month change is positive and UNRATE 3-month change is negative
evidence_refs: fact:UNRATE July 2005 vintage value was 5.0 percent
failure_condition: Reject if the joint PAYEMS-up and UNRATE-down state fails to produce positive 20-day returns in at least 55% of observations or has Rank IC below 0.03.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp decline in housing starts signals weakening interest-sensitive demand and future construction-related income pressure, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over 60 trading days.
candidate_trigger: HOUST falls more than 10% over 6 months
evidence_refs: fact:HOUST June 2005 vintage value was 2004 thousand units
failure_condition: Reject if 60-day returns following the HOUST decline trigger are not lower than unconditional returns or if the signal sign turns positive in two rolling 3-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: Higher 3-month industrial production growth signals improving real output and operating leverage for cyclicals, raising expected earnings and producing positive S&P 500 returns over 5 trading days.
failure_condition: Reject if higher INDPRO momentum does not generate positive 5-day return spreads after releases or if walk-forward Rank IC is below 0.03 for any 3-year window.

mechanism_narrative: A material rise in unemployment signals deteriorating labor demand and weaker household income expectations, reducing earnings forecasts and producing negative S&P 500 returns over 60 trading days.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE rises more than 0.3 percentage point over 3 months
evidence_refs: fact:UNRATE July 2005 vintage value was 5.0 percent
failure_condition: Reject if the unemployment-rise trigger is followed by positive 60-day S&P 500 returns in more than 60% of cases or if average triggered returns exceed baseline.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous growth in real GDP and retail sales identifies broad demand strength that supports aggregate earnings revisions, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over 60 trading days.
candidate_trigger: GDPC1 latest quarterly change is positive and RSAFS 3-month change is positive
evidence_refs: fact:GDPC1 April 2005 vintage value was 11092.0 billion chained 2017 dollars
failure_condition: Reject if the GDPC1-RSAFS joint-growth state has a nonpositive interaction coefficient or if 60-day forward returns are not above unconditional returns in walk-forward tests.
